title: Removing Consecutive Repeated Characters from a String with Edge Cases (Case Sensitive)

statement.markdown: Remove consecutive repeated characters from a string with edge cases like empty string, single character string, and strings with all consecutive repeated characters. The string should be case sensitive.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string of lowercase and uppercase English alphabets.
statement.outputFormat: The output should also be a string.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string will not contain any other characters than English alphabets.

examples.explanation: The longest substring without repeating characters is 'abc'.
The longest substring without repeating characters is an empty string.

hints.text: Start with a two-pointer technique and a set.

editorial.title: Optimal Solution
editorial.intuition: Use two pointers and a set to store unique characters.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves removing all duplicate characters, which is inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: Optimize by iterating through the input string once.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a set to store unique characters and two pointers to traverse the string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The algorithm correctly removes consecutive repeated characters.
editorial.dryRun: Start with an empty set and two pointers at the start of the string.

testCases.input: First line count, second line characters (stdin match)
